observations.summary: The tension between the desire to speak truthfully and the fear that one's words will be perceived as meaningless noise.

left_evidence.quote: He tried to say the simplest word—Mother—and the tongue went heavy in his mouth. Not silent. Not yet. But dense, forewarning, a cold thing lodged where certainty used to live.

right_evidence.quote: The terror was not the physical sensation. It was what it foretold: to be a speaker whose truth was only ever heard as noise.
